articles.title: Punk Rocker Baby Birds Win Big Photo Prize!
articles.summary: Imagine baby birds with spiky feathers that look just like punk rocker mohawks! That's the winning photo in the Smithsonian Magazine's 23rd annual contest. A veterinarian named Jacqueline Burke took the picture of three egret chicks in New Jersey. They were squabbling and pecking each other while their giant parent stood nearby. The photo is called "The Punk Rockers." Over 17,000 photos from 108 countries were submitted. Other winners include a woman in a black veil, a teen on a horse, and a colorful chameleon. The photos will be in the April/May 2026 issue of Smithsonian magazine.

articles.title: Peacocks Take Over a California Town!
articles.summary: Imagine walking out your front door and seeing a peacock strutting down the street! That's life for people in Arcadia, California. Dozens of peacocks live in this town, and some people love them while others really don't. The birds were brought there over 100 years ago by a businessman named Elias Lucky Baldwin. Now they walk on roofs, peck at cars, and show off their beautiful feathers. A filmmaker named Callie Barlow made a short movie about them called "Our Neighbors, the Peacocks." The movie shows how people have very different feelings about living with these fancy birds. Some want to protect them, others want them gone.

articles.title: Venice Art Show Jury Won't Give Prizes to Russia or Israel
articles.summary: The Venice Biennale is a huge art show that happens every two years in Italy. This year, the jury that gives out awards says it won't consider countries whose leaders are charged with crimes against humanity by the International Criminal Court. That means Russia and Israel can't win any prizes. The jury is made up of five women. Many artists and groups support this decision. Russia is returning to the show after missing it twice because of the war in Ukraine. Israel's participation has also caused protests. Nearly 10,000 people signed a letter asking for Russia to be kept out. The European Union even threatened to stop giving money to the Biennale.
